stories.headline: Far-Right Australian Party Wins Seat in First Election Victory
stories.summary: A far-right party won its first lower house election in Australia, with the victory underscoring the collapse in support for the center-right coalition that’s governed for much of the post-war period.

stories.headline: Iran war live: IRGC warns US against attacks on ships; Israel bombs Lebanon
stories.summary: US-Iran ceasefire holds as Tehran warns Washington against attacks on tankers and Israel kills 24 people in Lebanon.

stories.headline: Tehran, Taiwan, trade … what are the hazards facing Trump on Xi summit tightrope?
stories.summary: US leader enters talks with superpower rival from vulnerable position, but will be hoping for economic wins amid turbulent backdropIf all goes to plan over the next few days – and that is a big if – Donald Trump will arrive in Beijing on Wednesday for a highly anticipated summit with Xi Jinping, China’s leader.The trip will mark the first time a US president has visited China in nearly a decade. The last visit was also made by Trump, during his f

stories.headline: Australia news live: budget to include extra $2bn for infrastructure; teen dies after fall during Queensland police operation
stories.summary: Queensland police’s Ethical Standards Command investigating death in Spring Hill on Saturday evening. Follow updates liveWilson promises Coalition will be ‘very clear’ on migrationWilson is asked about comments made by the opposition leader, Angus Taylor, after the byelection that hinted at a rightward shift toward “ending mass migration” and stopping net zero policies.I can assure you in the coming weeks we’re going to make it very clear what we

stories.headline: Oil crisis sparks Japanese dash for corporate credit lines
China’s First-Quarter Marriage Registrations Fall to Lowest Ever
stories.summary: China’s marriage registrations in the first quarter fell to the lowest level on record for the three-month period, underscoring persistent weakness in the formation of new households — even during the peak wedding season.

stories.headline: The G.O.P. Rush To Break Up Majority-Black Districts
stories.summary: Republican-controlled legislatures in the South are breaking up majority-Black congressional districts in the wake of the Supreme Court’s recent ruling. Our national politics reporter Nick Corasaniti describes what it means for the midterms.

stories.headline: ‘No Kings’ protest outside Buckingham Palace
stories.summary: British anti-royals have staged a ‘No Kings’ protest outside Buckingham Palace in London.

stories.headline: China's aggressive squid fishing linked to sharp drops in catches
Remaking Hanoi: Vietnam bets on colossal capital renewal to drive growth
US investors cite 3 ways Japanese equities can unlock more value
One killed in Gaza as Turkish FM talks peace efforts with Hamas official
stories.summary: Israel continues to violate a 'ceasefire' agreed to in October with hundreds more killed across the Strip.

stories.headline: Russia will always be victorious, says Putin at scaled-back Victory Day parade
stories.summary: Moscow blanketed in heavy security despite last-minute announcement of three-day ceasefire with UkraineVladimir Putin has declared Russia will always be victorious as he oversaw a scaled-back Victory Day parade on Red Square held under heavy security amid mounting fears of Ukrainian attacks and growing public fatigue with the war.Speaking to the crowd, the Russian leader invoked the sacrifices of the second world war to rally support for his sold

stories.headline: US says the fragile truce with Iran holds as it awaits response to its latest ceasefire proposal
stories.summary: Washington insists its month-long ceasefire with Iran is continuing to hold despite renewed hostilities and the recent exchanges of fire. The US says it is awaiting Iran’s response to its latest ceasefire proposal to end the war.

stories.headline: Steve Rosenberg: This year's Victory Day parade in Moscow felt very different
stories.summary: The BBC's Russia editor reports from a Red Square with none of the military hardware which the Kremlin normally showcases to project power internationally.

stories.headline: Tenerife prepares to evacuate hantavirus cruise ship
stories.summary: Passengers with symptoms could be sent to isolation on the island, health officials warn

stories.headline: US Awaits Iranian Response After Hormuz Clashes Strain Ceasefire
stories.summary:
The US was waiting on Iran’s response to its latest proposal to end the war after suggesting word could come imminently, following clashes in the Strait of Hormuz that strained a month-long ceasefire. Bloomberg News Deputy UAE Bureau Chief Abeer Abu Omar and White House Correspondent Jeff Mason join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss.

Iran has still given no indication whether it will accept President Donald Tr

stories.headline: UK Moves Warship to Middle East for Potential Hormuz Mission
stories.summary: The UK will deploy one of its warships to the Middle East as part of planning for a European-led mission to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz once there’s a stable ceasefire.

stories.headline: ‘This is not another Covid,’ WHO chief tells Tenerife as hantavirus cruise ship heads to island – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closedThe interior minister of Spain told Reuters on Saturday that Germany, France, Belgium, Ireland and the Netherlands have confirmed they will send planes to repatriate nationals from their respective countries aboard the cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak.The European Union is sending two more planes for the remaining European citizens, and the US and UK have also confirmed planes and contingency plans for non-EU c

stories.headline: Putin vows victory in Ukraine and targets NATO at Moscow's scaled-back parade
stories.summary: In Moscow, a scaled-down Victory Day parade was held under tight security, without military hardware, amid fears of possible Ukrainian drone attacks. The Russian president denounced NATO in his speech.

stories.headline: Israeli settlers force Palestinian family to exhume and rebury their father
stories.summary: UN condemns confrontation in occupied West Bank as 'appalling and emblematic of the dehumanisation of Palestinians'.

stories.headline: Palestinians run West Bank freedom marathon along separation wall
stories.summary: Palestinians ran alongside the separation wall today, a structure that cuts them off from their land and their families.

stories.headline: Lebanon says Israeli strikes killed 39
stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah have continued to trade fire despite a ceasefire deal being announced last month.

stories.headline: UK warship HMS Dragon heads to Middle East for potential Strait of Hormuz mission
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence says the ship will prepare to join an international mission to safeguard shipping, but only when fighting in the region ends.

stories.headline: Rosenberg: Scaled-back Victory Parade in Moscow's Red Square
stories.summary: The BBC's Russia editor reports from Moscow, where the annual Victory Day display was quieter than usual.

stories.headline: DOJ Probing Suspicious Oil Trades Tied To Iran War
stories.summary: Chair of the SEC and CFTC Gary Gensler joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the US Justice Department and Commodity Futures Trading Commission are probe into at least four suspicious transactions in the oil market where traders made more than $2.6 billion. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran War Is Draining World’s Oil Buffer at an Unprecedented Pace
stories.summary: The world has burned through oil inventories at a record speed as the Iran war throttles flows from the Persian Gulf, eating into the very buffer that protects against supply shocks.

stories.headline: ‘It’s Not Because They Want to Be Friends’: Brilliant on What to Expect From the Trump-Xi Summit
stories.summary: The postponed Beijing summit between President Trump and China’s President Xi is back on the agenda, delayed by a war in Iran that has reshaped the dynamic between the two leaders. After a tumultuous 2025 that saw tariff escalation and revealed critical mineral choke points, both sides are looking for stability, not a breakthrough. Myron Brilliant, who recently returned from Beijing, says China thinks it's cracked the code on Trump. (Source: Bloo

stories.headline: With GOP wins on election maps, Democrats have a steeper climb to victory
stories.summary: Republicans are eight seats closer to keeping control of the House, making Democrats’ climb toward reclaiming power in November more difficult.

stories.headline: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
stories.summary: Jubilation in Budapest as new leader invites people to ‘step through gate of regime change’The pro-European centre-right leader Péter Magyar has been sworn in as prime minister of Hungary, marking the official end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power.Saturday’s ceremony – during which Magyar had invited people to join him to “write Hungarian history” together and “step through the gate of regime change” – comes a month after his opposition Tisza p

stories.headline: Hantavirus ship nears Tenerife as residents voice safety fears
stories.summary: The Spanish government has authorised passengers from the MV Hondius to disembark at the port of Granadilla this weekend following an agreement with the World Health Organisation. The decision has sparked protests from local dock workers in Santa Cruz.

stories.headline: Traders Looking for Next Leg in Global Stocks Rally Bet on Asia
stories.summary: As the focus moves away from the Iran war, investors and strategists alike are looking for the next leg up in equities. Many are turning to Asia.

stories.headline: Health Officials Downplay Pandemic Risk From Cruise Hantavirus Outbreak
stories.summary: The deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship doesn’t risk triggering the next pandemic, World Health Organization officials said, downplaying an incident that has raised concern about a new viral contagion. Former White House COVID Response Coordinator and Infectious Disease Expert Dr. Ashish Jha joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ‘A sobering indictment’: 14 homeless people die a year in public parks or countryside in Australia, analysis finds
stories.summary: ‘In just a few weeks homelessness has killed a baby, a young mother and a student,’ head of advocacy group says, calling for more investment in upcoming budgetFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastFourteen rough sleepers are dying in public parks or countryside areas each year on average in Australia, an analysis of hidden death reports reveals.The deaths of a young intern

stories.headline: ‘We’re coming after those other seats’: Pauline Hanson jubilant as One Nation wins Farrer byelection
stories.summary: David Farley secures One Nation’s first lower house win after Coalition vote collapses and Angus Taylor says Liberals will take ‘hard lessons’ from resultTom McIlroy: the scale of the Coalition’s loss in the Farrer byelection can’t be overstatedGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastPauline Hanson says One Nation is coming after Coalition and Labor seats around Australia, declaring her supporters want to “take the country back

stories.headline: Péter Magyar Takes Office, Marking the Official End of the Orbán Era
stories.summary: Peter Magyar has officially been sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister, almost a month after defeating Viktor Orban.

stories.headline: Péter Magyar sworn-in as Hungary's new prime minister after landslide April election victory
stories.summary: Péter Magyar called for those who occupy high office, described as remnants of the Orbán regime, to vacate their posts by the 31st of May. The order was extended to specifically include the country's President Tamás Sulyok.

stories.headline: ‘Triple whammy of climate chaos’: Why Antarctica's sea ice collapse is no longer a mystery
stories.summary: Between 2002 and 2020, Antarctica lost roughly 149 billion metric tonnes of ice each year, according to NASA.

stories.headline: Australia's right-wing One Nation party scores historic parliamentary win
stories.summary: One Nation gains its first seat in the lower house, in a by-election seen as a key test for the populist party.

stories.headline: Mexican parents criticise ending school year a month early for World Cup
stories.summary: The federal government faces widespread anger for saying the school year could end on 5 June to accommodate the football tournament.

stories.headline: Japanese council votes to remove unconscious mayor
stories.summary: Kikuo Hatakeyama, 72, has served as the mayor of Hachirogata in north-east Japan since 2008, but fell ill in February.

stories.headline: New York Stock Exchange to open private members’ club on Wall Street
stories.summary: Exclusive social hub planned for renovated vault as NYSE competes with Nasdaq for lucrative tech IPOs

stories.headline: Stock Trader’s Guide to Navigating High Stakes Trump-Xi Talks
stories.summary: Investors are looking for further signs of easing tensions between President Donald Trump and his counterpart Xi Jinping to help remove an overhang on Chinese markets, with geopolitical and trade issues in focus.

stories.headline: Australia to Tackle ‘Unacceptable’ Housing Market, Chalmers Says
stories.summary: Australia’s upcoming budget will take aim at soaring home prices and seek to make it easier for people to gain a foothold in the housing market, Treasurer Jim Chalmers said.

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 5/9/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Daniel Dae Kim, Host and Executive Producer of 'K-Everything,' Nicholas Burns, Former Ambassador to China and Professor of Diplomacy and International Relations at Harvard University, Gary Gensler, Former Chair, Commodity Futures Trading Commission

stories.headline: Why New Zealanders Are Moving To Australia in Record Numbers
stories.summary: About 41,000 New Zealanders moved to Australia in 2025, the most in 12 years, drawn by a more robust jobs market. When even former Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern left, it made international headlines. Net citizen migration is at its lowest since records began, and unlike in past waves, people aren't coming home. Former PM John Key, Cambridge Provost Gillian Tett, and a young lawyer who says he doubled his salary by leaving explain what's gone wron

stories.headline: China Vows to Advance Efforts to Defuse Local Government Debt
stories.summary: China pledged to step up efforts to defuse local government debt risk while supporting growth, as the State Council called for stronger policy execution in a challenging global environment.

stories.headline: Crypto Industry Throws Lap-Dance Party in Middle of Bear Market
stories.summary: People lined up for blocks and waited for hours to get into the E11even club in Miami on Wednesday night, with several men talking excitedly about the lap dances they planned to get inside.

stories.headline: How China May Have Sought to Recruit a House Aide to Spy on the U.S.
stories.summary: A staff member on the House China Committee was promised $10,000 for U.S. policy insights, on issues like Venezuela and rare-earth minerals.

stories.headline: Missing Singaporeans Located Near Mt. Dukono Crater Rim
stories.summary: Indonesian authorities locate missing Singaporeans after Mount Dukono eruption.

stories.headline: Syria and Lebanon make ‘significant progress’ at talks on joint interests
stories.summary: In Damascus, Lebanese prime minister and Syrian president discuss issues including security, transport and energy.

stories.headline: Parent company of Trump’s Truth Social site reports $400m loss this year
stories.summary: Losses driven largely by depreciation of cryptocurrency assets as prices of digital currencies tumble.

stories.headline: Kazakhstan strikes gold on day 2 in Astana
stories.summary: Day 2 of the Qazaqstan Barysy Grand Slam 2026 brought gold for the home crowd as Zhalgas Kairolla thrilled Astana. Enkhriilen Lkhagvatogoo, Danil Lavrentev and Clémence Eme also topped the podium, while Kazakhstan celebrated gold and bronze.

stories.headline: Cattle theft in Germany: organised gangs target farms
stories.summary: Livestock theft is becoming a problem in German agriculture, with entire herds disappearing overnight in some cases. In addition to financial losses, the offences also have an emotional impact on farmers. Investigators believe that organised gangs from abroad are involved.

stories.headline: Libya launches major security operation in Zawiya after clashes near key oil refinery
stories.summary: In Zawiya, home to one of the oil-rich country’s biggest refineries, rival gangs clash over the trafficking of fuel and commercial goods into neighbouring Tunisia.

stories.headline: Return of Palestine Marathon attracts thousands of runners
stories.summary: Thousands of people have taken part in the Palestine Marathon, which returns to Bethlehem after a two-year pause over the Gaza War.

stories.headline: Three dead after volcano erupts on Indonesian island
stories.summary: Officials had previously issued warnings on climbing the active volcano due to safety concerns.

stories.headline: Rubio charms in Rome, but he says Trump’s verbal attacks may continue
stories.summary: The secretary of state held warm meetings with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Pope Leo XIV after the president’s repeated criticism of both leaders.

stories.headline: Four south Florida men convicted in Haitian president’s assassination
stories.summary: Men were convicted in Miami federal court for plotting to kill Jovenel Moïse at his Port-au-Prince home in 2021Four south Florida men were convicted on Friday of plotting to kill the Haitian president, Jovenel Moïse, in 2021 by hiring mercenaries to assassinate him at his Port-au-Prince home, court records show.Prosecutors argued during the nine-week trial in a Miami federal court that the men assembled two dozen former Colombian soldiers and sup

stories.headline: From trusted aide to biggest rival: Suvendu Adhikari becomes West Bengal chief minister
stories.summary: A former ally of Mamata Banerjee, the BJP leader will be West Bengal’s next chief minister.

stories.headline: Facing Pressure, Trump Officials Reject Claims They’re Softening on Immigration
stories.summary: Immigration hard-liners have grown frustrated with the level of deportations and the Department of Homeland Security’s attempts, under its new secretary, to stay under the radar.

stories.headline: White House prepares to replace FDA leader Marty Makary amid agency turmoil
stories.summary: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary has faced criticism over his leadership of the agency that oversees vaccine and drug approvals.

stories.headline: Judge rules DOGE’s cuts to humanities grants were unconstitutional
stories.summary: A federal judge said more than $100 million in cuts to NEH grants were discriminatory, ruling in a case that revealed the inner workings of DOGE.

stories.headline: Hong Kong dissident Nathan Law on China spies in UK: ‘We’re not surprised’
stories.summary: Law says conviction of two men for spying raises serious concerns about how they accessed sensitive informationNathan Law, an exiled leader of the Hong Kong student protest who lives with a £100,000 bounty on his head from the Chinese authorities, was not surprised to discover a spy ring had photographed him entering the Oxford Union for an evening debate in November 2023.The conviction at the Old Bailey of Chi Leung “Peter” Wai, 38, and Chung Bi

stories.headline: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
stories.summary: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting because of the impasse in talks with Hamas.

stories.headline: Iran considering US proposal as Trump says war will be 'over quickly'
stories.summary: Mediator Pakistan says it is "endeavouring to convert this ceasefire into a permanent end to this war".

stories.headline: Trump's 'irresponsible war' to blame for economic slowdown, German minister says
stories.summary: Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil blamed the US president's "irresponsible war in Iran" for harming the Germany economy.

stories.headline: Anti-war protests rock Japan as PM pushes for stronger defence
stories.summary: Japan's largest anti-war protests in decades highlight a population divided over changes to its pacifist constitution.

stories.headline: Trump gives EU ultimatum deadline to approve trade deal with US
stories.summary: Trump's deadline to the European Union came as a trade court ruled his global tariff policy violated US law.

stories.headline: Disturbances and 127 arrests mar Paris party after PSG Champions League victory
stories.summary: Fans took to the streets to celebrate the Paris team's qualification for the final, but celebrations were marred by disturbances.

stories.headline: China announces suspended death sentences for former defence ministers
stories.summary: The announcement follows recent ousting of several top officials in China's military.

stories.headline: Crowds cheer China's new snooker star on return from championship win
stories.summary: Wu Yize, 22, receives a rock star's welcome after becoming second-youngest player to win a world title.

stories.headline: Top BJP leader's aide shot dead in violence after Indian state election
stories.summary: Chandranath Rath was personal assistant to Suvendu Adhikari, front runner to become West Bengal's new chief minister.

stories.headline: Woman jailed in Somalia for peaceful protest ‘stripped, kicked and beaten’
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview from prison, Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old rickshaw driver, tells of her treatment after being arrested for demonstrating against the governmentA woman being held in prison in Somalia for taking part in peaceful protests has described how she was tortured by her guards.Sadia Moalim Ali, 27, told the Guardian she was stripped naked by two male guards in a room monitored by CCTV, kicked, beaten with a baton and left for

stories.headline: 'Enjoy the show. Ignore the war': Venice Biennale faces backlash after including Russia
stories.summary: Protests have taken place at the prestigious arts fair over the inclusion of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: Not all oil giants are prospering from the Iran war
stories.summary: Exxon and Chevron have benefited less than their European rivals

stories.headline: China calls for Strait to be reopened 'as soon as possible' in Iran talks
stories.summary: Wang Yi is holding talks with Iran's foreign minister Aragchi who is on his first trip to China since the war began.

stories.headline: What is Trump's 'Project Freedom' in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: The US president said the operation has been paused after starting on Monday.

stories.headline: Only one of Berkshire Hathaway and SoftBank can survive
stories.summary: The two represent competing visions of the future

stories.headline: Bowen: Strait of Hormuz standoff raises risk of sliding back into all-out war
stories.summary: The US and Iran's determination to keep the pressure on each other has put the fragile Gulf ceasefire in serious jeopardy.

stories.headline: Nine coal miners die in gas explosion in Colombia
stories.summary: The explosion is the latest accident in the locality of Sutatausa, the site of another deadly mine disaster in 2023.

stories.headline: Global carmakers desperately want to be more Chinese
stories.summary: But partnering with local companies carries big risks

stories.headline: The remarkable revival of eBay
stories.summary: The internet’s flea market is back—and GameStop wants to buy it
